pages.full_text:
We got to the Tunnel an hour early & they put us onto the next train - ahead of the Bank Holiday weekend.
We drove past Etaples & Rouen & are camping beyond Dreux at a Municipal Park at St. Remysur l'Avre. The river splits in 2 in the town.
A lovely, [?&] warm evening walk in the sunshine. There's an Irishman in the camp who sits in his car all day.
Delicious
[?eclair/eclairs]
[?1/3] fields oil seed rape
[remainder cut off / illegible at bottom]
pages.notes: Page is a continuation from preceding spread. Top of page text begins mid-sentence. Left side features a hand-drawn sketch of a whisk and a glued-in rubbing/print of a baguette. Bottom text is partially cut off; 'eclair/eclairs' is a best guess. The place name 'St. Remysur l'Avre' may read 'St. Rémy-sur-Avre' — written as one word in the diary. 'Etaples' could be 'Étaples'. No handwritten page number visible (top corner cut off or absent).

pages.full_text:
[?...] Saturday.
      We shopped in Dreux & lunched overlooking R. le [?hour] a few kilometres north of Chateaudun. We've camped at le Floe at Beaugency. lots of watermills, broad river. Old bridges. Church has a capitol of David & Goliath & font from 1593. A good meal (except pudding). A nice town to wander in.

      Lovely views from the van across the river.

Sunday a near-perfect day so far.
Warm sunshine in the van first thing. An 11 mile bike ride to Meung. lovely bike track. Fields, villages.
Delicious lasagna, rose &
lashings of chocolate on
the profiteroles at this
restaurant. Frogs making a
tremendous racket.
A market in Meung & beautful Romanesque door outside Church & nice Stained glass window.
Back to Beaugency. The bridge is beautiful.
Both campsites have been only €11 per night.
pages.notes: Page begins mid-entry with '[?...] Saturday.' — continuation from left page or previous spread. 'R. le [?hour]' likely refers to a river name (possibly 'R. le Loir'). 'le Floe' is uncertain — could be 'le Floc' or similar campsite name at Beaugency. 'Chateaudun' likely 'Châteaudun'. 'Sunday' is underlined. Restaurant card 'L'Atelier de Jeanne, 3 impasse du Château – Meung sur Loire' is glued in on the right side. Euro currency (€11) confirms date is post-2002; volume context (2003–2019) supports this. 'beautful' is as written (missing 'i').
